name: Source Fidelity review for Telomerase Restoration TA-65 metabolic results repair bundle
summary: The staged source, study, finding, and outlook changes are source-faithful. ClinicalTrials.gov identifies NCT02531334 as the University of Connecticut H14-278 randomized double-masked crossover TA-65 metabolic-syndrome trial with estimated enrollment of 40, actual completion in June 2018, and hasResults=false. PubMed identifies PMID 29546832 as the 2018 Current Pharmaceutical Design randomized controlled TA-65 metabolic-syndrome article, and the full text explicitly links the study to H14-278 and ClinicalTrials.gov protocol NCT02531334. The staged result claims match the abstract/full-text biomarker results and correctly preserve the no-posted-registry-table boundary.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 29546832 is correctly matched to NCT02531334 through the full-text H14-278 and ClinicalTrials.gov protocol references, with matching title, sponsor context, 40-person enrollment, randomized crossover design, 16 mg/day TA-65 dosing, and 12-week treatment periods separated by washout.
findings.why_it_matters: The trial-watch state should change only if the publication is truly the registry-linked result source rather than a similar TA-65 study.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; treat PMID 29546832 as the result-bearing source for NCT02531334.

findings.claim_or_issue: The registry still has hasResults=false while the publication carries outcome results, so the staged split between results_status not_posted and watch_status results_captured is faithful.
findings.why_it_matters: This preserves registry transparency without leaving the study in the misleading active late-no-results retirement queue.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the registry no-posted-table finding separate from the publication result finding.

findings.claim_or_issue: The full text states that TA-Sciences prepared the capsules and that randomization was done by the company, while the staged finding caveats do not mention this trial-specific company role.
findings.why_it_matters: Commercial involvement can affect confidence in a small supplement trial, especially when the result is being added to public support maps.
findings.recommended_action: This does not block approval because the staged claim is already low-confidence, disease-context, and biomarker-only; editorial may add a trial-specific company-role caveat if it wants the new finding to carry the conflict boundary directly.
